title: Analitiq canonical types
description: Analitiq's profile of the Apache Arrow logical type system. Canonical types are strings that identify an Arrow logical type in the form used by `type_map.canonical` entries on connector definitions. Primitive types are bare identifiers (`Utf8`, `Int64`, `Boolean`). Parameterized types use PascalCase with parenthesized arguments (`Decimal128(18, 4)`, `Timestamp(MICROSECOND, UTC)`). Nested types use angle brackets (`List<Int32>`, `Struct<id:Int64, name:Utf8>`, `Map<Utf8, Int64>`). Reference: https://arrow.apache.org/docs/format/Columnar.html#logical-types. Scope note: patterns for nested types (List/Struct/Map/Union/Dictionary/RunEndEncoded) validate the outer shape only; deep validation of inner canonical types is the validator API's responsibility, not JSON Schema's. Version note: the engine pins PyArrow to ^12.0.0, so Arrow 15+ view types (`Utf8View`, `BinaryView`, `ListView`, `LargeListView`) are intentionally excluded from this vocabulary. If the engine upgrades to Arrow 15 or later, extend this file.

canonical_type.title: canonical_type
canonical_type.description: A canonical type string. Must match one of the Arrow logical type families below.

null_type.title: Null
null_type.description: Arrow Null logical type. Column contains only null values. Zero storage.

boolean_type.title: Boolean
boolean_type.description: Arrow Boolean logical type. 1-bit values: true or false.

integer_type.title: Int / UInt
integer_type.description: Arrow signed and unsigned integer logical types at fixed widths of 8, 16, 32, or 64 bits. Width should reflect the source system's declared width — do not upcast `Int32` to `Int64` unless the engine requires it.

floating_type.title: FloatingPoint
floating_type.description: Arrow IEEE-754 floating-point logical types. Float16 is half-precision; Float32 is single-precision; Float64 is double-precision.

binary_type.title: Binary
binary_type.description: Arrow binary logical types. `Binary` is variable-length with 32-bit offsets; `LargeBinary` is variable-length with 64-bit offsets; `FixedSizeBinary(byte_width)` is fixed-length.

string_type.title: Utf8
string_type.description: Arrow UTF-8 string logical types. `Utf8` uses 32-bit offsets (capped ~2 GiB total per array); `LargeUtf8` uses 64-bit offsets.

date_type.title: Date
date_type.description: Arrow Date logical types. `Date32` is days since the Unix epoch; `Date64` is milliseconds since the Unix epoch (must be a multiple of 86_400_000).

time_type.title: Time
time_type.description: Arrow time-of-day logical types. `Time32` supports SECOND and MILLISECOND units; `Time64` supports MICROSECOND and NANOSECOND units.

timestamp_type.title: Timestamp
timestamp_type.description: Arrow Timestamp logical type. Unit is one of SECOND, MILLISECOND, MICROSECOND, NANOSECOND. Timezone is either `null` (zone-naive / local) or a string — IANA zone (`UTC`, `America/New_York`), fixed offset (`+05:30`, `-08:00`), or similar. When mapping from a source that declares a zoned type (e.g., Postgres `TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE`), use `UTC` unless there is a source-specific reason otherwise.

duration_type.title: Duration
duration_type.description: Arrow Duration logical type. Elapsed time with unit SECOND, MILLISECOND, MICROSECOND, or NANOSECOND.

interval_type.title: Interval
interval_type.description: Arrow Interval logical type. YEAR_MONTH stores months as int32; DAY_TIME stores days + milliseconds as two int32s; MONTH_DAY_NANO stores months + days + nanoseconds in 16 bytes.

decimal_type.title: Decimal
decimal_type.description: Arrow Decimal128 or Decimal256 logical type. `Decimal128(precision, scale)` supports precision 1-38; `Decimal256(precision, scale)` supports precision 1-76. Scale may be negative but is typically 0 or positive. Use `Decimal128` unless the source precision exceeds 38.

list_type.title: List / LargeList / FixedSizeList
list_type.description: Arrow list logical types. `List<T>` uses 32-bit offsets; `LargeList<T>` uses 64-bit offsets; `FixedSizeList<T>[n]` has fixed list length `n`. `T` is itself a canonical type; inner-type validation is performed by the validator API.

struct_type.title: Struct
struct_type.description: Arrow Struct logical type. Fields are `name:type` pairs separated by commas: `Struct<id:Int64, name:Utf8, created:Timestamp(MICROSECOND, UTC)>`. Field names follow the source field naming; no case normalization is performed.

map_type.title: Map
map_type.description: Arrow Map logical type with key and value types: `Map<Utf8, Int64>`. Arrow maps are logically unordered; engines may sort keys for efficient lookup.

union_type.title: Union
union_type.description: Arrow Union logical type. `SparseUnion<T1, T2, ...>` stores one buffer per child type, full-length; `DenseUnion<T1, T2, ...>` stores child buffers at length equal to each type's usage count, plus an offsets buffer. Use sparse for few types or uniform distribution; dense for many types with skewed distribution.

dictionary_type.title: Dictionary
dictionary_type.description: Arrow Dictionary logical type. `Dictionary<index_type, value_type>` stores integer indices referencing a shared dictionary of values. Use for low-cardinality string/binary columns to reduce storage.

run_end_encoded_type.title: RunEndEncoded
run_end_encoded_type.description: Arrow RunEndEncoded logical type. `RunEndEncoded<run_end_type, values_type>` stores consecutive repeated values as (run_end_index, value) pairs. Efficient for columns with long runs of identical values.

canonical_ssl_mode.title: canonical_ssl_mode
canonical_ssl_mode.description: Canonical SSL/TLS mode for database connections. `none` disables TLS; `require` mandates TLS but performs no certificate verification; `verify-ca` mandates TLS and verifies the certificate chain only (hostname not checked — typical for internal CAs, proxies, or deliberately relaxed setups); `verify-full` mandates TLS and verifies both chain and hostname; `prefer` attempts TLS and falls back to plaintext if unavailable. Values mirror Postgres `sslmode` / MySQL `--ssl-mode` vocabulary so `ssl-mode-map.json` authoring from driver docs is mostly 1:1. Used only by SSL-capable connectors (databases that support TLS). API and storage connectors do not carry an `ssl_mode_map`.
